entry.title: AI and UX sessions at SAP Sapphire 2026 that I’m most excited about
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is where SAP makes its big announcements about its vision for the future of ERP and business applications. This includes showing new products, presenting customers who have transformed their business using SAP’s solutions, and highlighting new technologies that will shape the future.

For 2026, AI will continue to dominate the event. AI will change the way that people consume enterprise software through chat-based interfaces and screens that generate on-the-fly based on what you want to do. Eventually.

In this post, I’ll share the sessions at Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference that I’m most excited about. For the full menu, you can browse the session catalog. My preferences are, of course, shaped by my role on the Cloud ERP UX Foundation team. I’ll organize by date so you can plan your agenda. You can also use the browser to search by topic or speaker if that makes it easier for you. Some are even available on-demand. Let’s go!

Monday, May 11, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Monday
10am – 5pm
ASUG102

Unlocking Business Value with AI: Strategy, Security, and the Road Ahead

Gain a comprehensive and practical overview of SAP Business AI. This workshop will examine core strategies, security frameworks, and future trends while exploring real-world customer stories. The session will conclude with an expert panel and a hands-on AI road mapping session.

Many experts from SAP

Monday
2:00pm – 2:20pm
ERP2143

The future of enterprise: Innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private

Discover how SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions enable the next era of enterprise transformation. This introductory session explores how cutting-edge artificial intelligence, trusted data, and integrated applications work in harmony, creating smarter, faster, and more resilient businesses within the security and flexibility of your private cloud.

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Vinay V, SAP

Monday
2:30pm – 2:50pm
CXP2149

Legacy to legendary: The AI-enhanced path

Explore the strategic road map for moving from legacy on-premises systems to a cloud-native, AI-enhanced architecture. Discover how a clean core approach helps reduce technical debt, accelerate innovation, and position your organization to use AI for greater efficiency and agility.

 

Monday
3:30pm – 3:50pm
PUX1349

Innovations and value that SAP Business AI offers

Learn how you can find the best AI features for your use cases, and understand the business value and associated cost with our self-service tools.

Pascal Mauge, SAP

 

Tuesday, May 12, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Tuesday
10:30 am - 11:15 am PUX1228

Creating better user experiences with AI-assisted SAP Fiori apps | PUX1228

New AI features in SAP Fiori apps boost user productivity and speed onboarding. Starting with the “My Home” page and drilling down into typical SAP Fiori apps, see how AI makes it easier to find information, enter data, automate tasks, and make informed business decisions. Explore how conversational interactions make SAP solutions more intuitive.

 

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

Tuesday
11:00 am - 11:20 am PUX1346

Great features don’t guarantee success: Experience does

Explore SAP Business Suite in the cloud to discover how you can shape the experience of the product for stellar adoption success.

Daniel Becker, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm FOU1861

ASUG Community Conversation: Artificial intelligence

Explore platform strategy, embedded and generative AI use cases, integration patterns, and road map priorities with experts in AI and SAP Business Technology Platform. Discuss architecture, data readiness, and clean core alignment while exchanging lessons learned with peers advancing AI innovation.

John Astill, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm ERP1622

SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA: What’s new, why change, and why now?

 Learn from more than 2,400 cloud ERP projects as we navigate the shift from SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) to SAP S/4HANA. Compare greenfield, brownfield, and selective data transitions to find the right path. Discover the key differences in SAP S/4HANA, the value of moving now, and how to accelerate AI adoption with modern technology.

 

Esther Vrieze, SAP

Bert Schulze, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm BTM2345

Why ERP transformations fail: The change management gap

Organizations expect operational efficiency and digital capabilities from their SAP solution transformations, yet some ERP programs fail to realize full value. The missing link is often adoption. See how integrating organizational change management early in the SAP lifecycle reduces resistance, accelerates adoption, and improves business outcomes.

 

Kara Beck, Moog Inc.

Tuesday
1:00 pm - 1:45 pm ERP1864

ASUG Community Conversation: Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA

In this interactive ASUG Community Conversation for professionals in cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA, explore strategies across migration, operation, and innovation—featuring our road maps, clean core and AI insights, and customer lessons learned. Exchange practical guidance with peers and shape future discussions through open dialogue.

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Tuesday
1:00 pm - 1:20 pm JOU2645

The future of enterprise applications and AI experiences

Unlock real value from agentic AI with insights from SAP experts and peers. Explore what transformation means today and how you can scale AI across the business, overcome common challenges, govern risk, and build an AI-first enterprise that drives measurable top- and bottom-line impact.

 

Tuesday
2:00 pm - 2:45 pm ERP1178

2026 and beyond: Discover the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private 

Running your ERP in the cloud allows you to benefit from innovations at your own pace. Explore recent AI and other functional developments planned for the next releases of SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions to help you prepare for adoption, build your road map, and accelerate time to value.

Maura Hameroff, SAP

Rudolf Hois, SAP

Tuesday
3:00 pm - 3:45 pm FOU1559

Build enterprise-grade pro-code agents on SAP Business Technology Platform

Unlock the power of autonomous agents with enterprise-grade guarantees. Get technical tips on building pro-code agents on SAP Business Technology Platform that balance innovation with responsibility. Learn to use the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for smooth tool integration while supporting security, ethical AI behavior, and regulatory compliance.

Evgenii Skrebtcov, SAP

Tim Back, SAP

Tuesday
3:00 pm - 3:45 pm ERP1834

Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping a clean core | ERP1834

Extend the capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud to address your business needs. Use AI to generate projects in the ABAP programming language and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model from your business requirements. Build SAP Fiori apps based on your written or graphical description, and use natural language filtering to find information in them.

Katja Zoch, SAP

Tuesday
3:30 pm - 3:50 pm JOU2646

AI as the new UI

The interface is disappearing, and that's the point. As AI becomes the primary way people interact with enterprise software, the question is no longer how to design better screens but how to deliver better outcomes. Intent replaces navigation, conversation replaces configuration, and the most powerful user experience is the one you barely notice.

 

Tuesday
4:00 pm - 4:20 pm PUX1192

SAP's user experience strategy for an AI-powered web and mobile future

Get an overview of how SAP is helping business users get their work done more easily and with better outcomes: using the Joule solution and embedded AI to take UX to the next level, providing a consistent and integrated suite experience, and leveraging our user research framework to meet users' needs. Also see how you can create great UX yourself.

Arin Bhowmick, SAP

 

Wednesday, May 13, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Wednesday
10:30 am – 11:15 am SAP1623

SAP ECC to SAP Business Suite: Migration strategies and cloud insights

Master the direct transition from SAP ERP Central Component to SAP Business Suite using proven best practices. Compare public, private, and on-premises deployments to define key decision drivers. Learn to navigate annual releases, leverage essential migration tools, and identify the evolving skills needed for a successful, seamless cloud journey.

Esther Vrieze, SAP

Bert Schultze, SAP

Wednesday
10:30 am – 11:15 am PUX1819

SAP Fiori in action: How modern UX drives efficiency in cloud ERP

Get an overview of how the SAP Fiori user experience (UX) helps users get their work done more easily and efficiently. See how we are using AI today in applications and with the Joule solution. Understand our road map for revolutionizing UX, how AI changes the idea of what an application is, and what all this means for you.

Thomas Reiss, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 11:50 am SUS1523

AI that helps you operate your business

As sustainability shifts from reporting to real business decisions, it becomes a control and execution challenge. Explore how you can replace manual processes with AI for trusted, scalable insights and decision support, delivering accountability for operational and financial decision-making.

Sophia Mendelsohn, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm PUX1195

Creating AI-powered UX for your own applications with SAP Design System

Learn how to create AI-powered UX for your applications by using SAP Design System (SAP Fiori UX for web and mobile apps) with its powerful set of reusable components, patterns, and floorplans. See how building applications is made easier with our AI-enabled UI technologies for web and mobile apps.

Sophia Levens, SAP

Frederic Berg, SAP

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm ERP2238

Western Sugar’s success with SAP Cloud ERP

Five years after go-live, Western Sugar Cooperative calls SAP Cloud ERP applications “wonderful.” Hear about why it chose public cloud; the value of clean core, best practices, strong configuration and integration, and continuous improvement; its smooth move from a two- to three-system landscape; and readiness for AI-enabled innovation.

 

Wednesday
11:30 am – 12:15 pm FOU2337

When AI becomes the interface to the enterprise

For decades, SAP helped shape enterprise software based on screens and transactions. Today, AI is evolving how we interact with systems. Explore how SAP leads this shift—uniting applications, data, and AI—and what this next phase of transformation means for organizations turning AI into a real competitive advantage.

Florian Heretsch, SAP

Wednesday
1:00 pm – 1:45 pm FOU1557

Getting started with SAP Build MCPs: Create your first CAP app and process

 Experience the powerful Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration in SAP Build solutions. Produce your first SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) application and SAP Fiori elements UI, while orchestrating workflows. See how AI-enhanced context improves coding efficiency and helps you build scalable, process-rich apps.

 

Tim Back, SAP

Jason Gabor, SAP

Wednesday
1:00 pm – 1:45 pm BTM1232

The AI-augmented architect: Upgrading architecture in the age of AI 

 AI is reshaping enterprise architecture. To provide value, enterprise architectures must embrace AI tools. Discover SAP technology leveraging AI for enhanced visibility and coordination. Through real-world examples, see how Joule and agentic AI increase information velocity and augment architect capabilities to thrive in this evolving landscape.

Martin Mysyk, SAP

Wednesday
2:00 pm – 2:20 pm PUX1210

AI and the future of user experience in SAP software

Prepare yourself for the upcoming revolution in user experience by understanding how we are harnessing AI to help users achieve better outcomes. Discover how AI is changing the idea of what an application is—and what this means for you. Learn about our AI design patterns and get a glimpse of the future SAP software user experience.

Emil Voutta, SAP

Wednesday
2:00 pm – 2:20 pm JOU2647

The future of enterprise applications and AI experiences

The enterprise application is no longer a system of record but a system of action. As AI moves from assistant to agent, organizations shift from what their software can store to what it can do. Learn why the future belongs to applications that don't just surface information but understand context, anticipate decisions, and act with confidence.

 

Wednesday
3:00 pm – 3:20 pm PUX1194

User experience innovations and road map for SAP Business Suite

Have a look at the latest user experience innovations across our suite of applications, along with exciting web and mobile innovations coming soon. Also check out selected highlights from Ul technologies for creating great UX for your own applications.

Tom Reiss, SAP

I’m excited to hear what we announce in the keynotes and experience the future of enterprise software. AI will transform how we all interact with apps. The future starts now.

Here are some other summaries of Sapphire sessions from other colleagues.

Inside SAP Build at SAP Sapphire 2026: What to Expect from AI Agents and Agentic Development by Mark Wright

See you in Orlando!

For the SAP Cloud ERP UX Foundation team, Peter Spielvogel.

entry.title: Service in SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025
entry.content:
• SAP Cloud ERP Private for Service 2025 FPS1: What's New
  • Search, navigate and update service contracts with Joule
  • Joule for transferring in-house service objects
  • Direct maintenance plan creation from the service contract
  • Enriching service contract header & item with multilevel categorization
  • Advance shipment stock monitor
  • Advanced execution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for Service solution: OData API support for billable maintenance order
  • Applying lists of serial numbers to in-house service objects
  • Intercompany repair and logistics for in-house service
  • Process flow in service transactions at the header level
  • Service team and enterprise service organization determination
  • Service with advanced execution: change of profit center in billable maintenance order
  • Service with advanced execution: RRCO subitems in Service Order
  • Service with advanced execution: changing capability for billable maintenance order
  • Splitting and merging in-house service objects
  • Data product for service configuration data in SAP Business Data Cloud
  • New BAdI in Recurring Service

SAP Cloud ERP Private for Service 2025 FPS1: What's New

Service in SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1 brings a rich set of innovations across service contract management, in-house service, service order management, and AI-powered experiences. Here is a walkthrough of every new feature in this release.

 

Search, navigate and update service contracts with Joule

SAP Joule now extends its AI capabilities into service contract management. Users can search for service contracts using natural language queries — specifying parameters such as sold-to party, contact person, currency, status, or time frame — and navigate directly to the contract details without manually filtering through lists. Joule can also update the status of a service contract to "Released" or "Completed" through a simple conversational request, removing the need to open and manually change the document.

 

 

Service teams can find exactly what they need in seconds and act on it without leaving the Joule interface. This eliminates repetitive navigation steps, reduces the risk of missing contract updates, and empowers service managers to stay on top of contract lifecycles — all through natural language, with no deep system knowledge required.

 

Joule for transferring in-house service objects

Joule can now assist with the logistics of in-house service — specifically the transfer of service objects between plants and storage locations. Using a conversational approach, users can ask Joule to create the delivery required to move a service object, specifying the target plant and storage location as part of the dialogue. Joule also supports creating inbound deliveries for receiving in-house service objects, making both the outbound transfer and the receipt sides of the process available through the same chatbot interface.

 

Initiating the transfer of a service object no longer requires navigating through multiple logistics transactions. Technicians and service coordinators can trigger goods movements conversationally, reducing manual steps and the chance of errors. This makes the in-house repair workflow faster and more accessible, especially for users who are less familiar with the underlying logistics processes.

 

Direct maintenance plan creation from the service contract

This feature tightens the integration between service contracts and maintenance planning. When working with service contract templates, users can now add an existing maintenance plan as a copy-template reference on the template item. When a service contract is created from that template, the referenced maintenance plan is automatically copied. Upon releasing a service contract item, a new maintenance plan is generated from that reference — ready to drive recurring service execution. For contracts not based on templates, users can also add a maintenance plan copy reference directly on the service contract item.

 

 

Organizations managing large service contracts with many recurring service items no longer need to create maintenance plans separately and link them manually. The entire process becomes automated at the point of contract release, significantly reducing the administrative effort for service contract managers and ensuring that preventive maintenance schedules are always aligned with the contractual commitments.

 

Enriching service contract header & item with multilevel categorization

Service contracts now support a multilevel categorization framework, allowing up to four category levels to be assigned at both the header and item level. This taxonomy is configurable and directly influences downstream processes. The most powerful downstream effect is "auto complete": when a categorization is applied, service contract items can be automatically populated from a matching service contract template, reducing manual data entry during contract creation.

 

A standardized categorization taxonomy across service contracts makes it easier to analyze service data, enforce process compliance, and drive automation. Organizations can segment their service contract portfolio consistently, enable smarter reporting, and speed up contract creation through auto-completion — all of which improve the quality of data feeding into operational and financial decisions.

 

Advance shipment stock monitor

The new "Monitor Advance Shipment Stock" SAP Fiori app gives service organizations full visibility into service parts that have been shipped in advance to customers from service orders. The app displays each service order alongside the relevant parts, showing ordered quantity, delivered quantity, consumed quantity, and unutilized quantity — all filterable by plant, material, sold-to party, service order status, and service organization.

 

Knowing exactly how many pre-shipped parts have been consumed versus how many remain unused at customer sites is critical for inventory control and billing accuracy. This app eliminates the guesswork, allowing service managers to proactively follow up on unconsumed parts, avoid write-offs, and ensure that billing reflects actual consumption — improving both cash flow and customer satisfaction.

 

Advanced execution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for Service solution: OData API support for billable maintenance order

The standard SAP Enterprise Asset Management OData API `API_MAINTENANCEORDER` has been extended to support billable maintenance orders as used in the SAP S/4HANA Service solution with advanced execution. Specifically, the API now supports changing a billable maintenance order, enabling programmatic updates to these documents through the same standard interface used for regular maintenance orders.

 

Customers and partners who have built integrations or automation on top of the standard maintenance order API can now extend those solutions to cover billable maintenance orders without developing custom interfaces. This reduces integration complexity and maintenance overhead, and enables seamless end-to-end automation for service processes that combine the service billing logic with asset management execution.

 

Applying lists of serial numbers to in-house service objects

In-house service objects with a quantity greater than one can now have a list of serial numbers assigned directly to them. These serial number lists are also transferred between the in-house service object and the associated logistics transactions — including customer returns and delivery documents — keeping serial number tracking consistent across the entire in-house service process.

 

 

Previously, handling multiple serialized items in a single in-house service transaction required creating separate service objects for each serial number, multiplying the number of documents to manage. This enhancement allows teams to consolidate serialized items under a single service object, reducing document volume, simplifying workflows, and aligning in-house service with the standard serial number handling conventions used across SAP S/4HANA logistics.

 

Intercompany repair and logistics for in-house service

Organizations can now route in-house service objects to repair centers in a different company code. Users can trigger intercompany goods movements directly from the "Manage Service Objects" and "Manage In-House Service" apps for service objects in customer stock. An intercompany service order manages the financial settlement between the commercial company and the receiving company, and a linked in-house service is created in the receiving company that follows the same process as a standard in-house service.

 

 

Service organizations with specialized repair centers in different legal entities — whether in the same country or across borders — can now execute those repairs within a single, integrated SAP process. The financial flow between the entities is handled automatically via the intercompany service order, and full transparency into the in-house service status is available in both the commercial and receiving company. This eliminates manual workarounds and reduces the complexity of managing cross-entity service operations.

 

Process flow in service transactions at the header level

The graphical process flow visualization — already available on individual items — is now displayed at the header level of service quotations and service orders. The process flow covers the full chain: Service Quotation → Service Order → Billable Maintenance Order → Service Confirmation → Billing Document. Each document in the chain is shown with its status and key details, giving users an at-a-glance view of where a transaction stands end-to-end.

 

Instead of drilling into individual items to understand where a service transaction is in its lifecycle, users can now get the full picture from the header level in one view. This reduces the time needed to answer status questions, supports faster decision-making during service execution, and makes it easier to spot bottlenecks or missing follow-up documents across the entire order-to-billing process.

 

Service team and enterprise service organization determination

Several new determination mechanisms have been introduced to make service team and enterprise service organization assignment more flexible and automated. The service team can now be determined by team member based on the partner function in a service transaction. The enterprise service organization can then be derived from an attribute of the determined service team, or via a new BAdI for custom determination logic. A new customizing profile and access sequence enable these determination rules to be activated per transaction type, and the enterprise service organization can be made obligatory through that profile.

 

 

 

Setting up service teams and organizational assignments is one of the more complex configuration areas in SAP Service. These enhancements reduce that complexity by enabling automatic derivation of the enterprise service organization from the service team — and by providing a BAdI for edge cases — meaning fewer manual entries in transactions, more consistent organizational assignments, and less time spent on setup and maintenance.

 

Service with advanced execution: change of profit center in billable maintenance order

Users can now control profit center assignment independently on the billable maintenance order (cost side) versus the execution order item (revenue side). A new customizing option on the service order item category determines whether the profit center value is transferred from the service order to the billable maintenance order, or whether it can be set independently. When enabled, users can manually edit the profit center directly on the billable maintenance order.

 

In complex intracompany service scenarios, cost and revenue may need to be reported against different profit centers for accurate internal accounting. This feature gives finance and service operations teams the flexibility to model those scenarios correctly within SAP S/4HANA Service with advanced execution, without resorting to manual journal entries or workarounds — improving the integrity of cost and profitability reporting.

 

Service with advanced execution: RRCO subitems in Service Order

Resource-Related Cost Objects (RRCO) subitems — previously only available in service quotations — can now also exist in service orders when using the advanced execution scenario. These subitems can be taken over from a service quotation when creating a service order, copied from an existing quotation, or generated using the "Refresh Planning" button directly in the service order. Note that credit check is not supported for RRCO subitems.

 

RRCO subitems provide an alternative path for calculating the PCO4 cost condition and planned revenue on execution order items, which directly influences Estimate at Completion (EBRR) calculations. This gives project-oriented service scenarios greater accuracy in cost and revenue planning, and enables a more consistent flow of financial data from service orders into controlling — supporting better margin visibility across complex service engagements.

 

Service with advanced execution: changing capability for billable maintenance order

Users can now make changes to billable maintenance orders directly from the SAP Fiori apps used for asset management — including "Find Maintenance Orders", "Find Maintenance Notifications and Orders", and "Find Maintenance Orders and Operations". Mass changes affecting certain fields are also supported across multiple billable maintenance orders at once. In addition, the SAPGui mass-change transactions IW72, IW38, and IW37N now support billable maintenance orders, and the BAPI `BAPI_ALM_ORDER_MAINTAIN` has been extended to change billable maintenance orders and to set the billable flag on an existing maintenance order.

 

Service operations running on the advanced execution model now have the full range of change and mass-change capabilities available — whether working in the modern Fiori interface or in classic SAPGui transactions. This removes the need for workarounds when bulk updates are required, significantly reducing the time and effort needed to manage large volumes of billable maintenance orders and enabling tighter integration with existing asset management workflows.

 

Splitting and merging in-house service objects

The "Manage In-House Service" app now includes a Restructure function that allows users to split a single in-house service object into multiple new service objects, or merge several service objects into one. The original service objects are documented on the "Manage Service Objects Details" screen, providing a traceable history of any restructuring.

 

 

 

 

When a batch of returned items needs to be sent to different repair locations, splitting them into separate service objects ensures each can follow its own repair path. Conversely, when large numbers of service objects clutter the app, merging them simplifies the view and reduces document management overhead. Both capabilities improve operational clarity and flexibility in how in-house service workloads are organized and executed.

 

Data product for service configuration data in SAP Business Data Cloud

A new "service configuration data" data product is now available in SAP Business Data Cloud for customers running SAP S/4HANA Service. This data product exposes the configuration data — including SAP-defined code list values and customer-configured settings — that governs service management processes, establishing a unified data foundation that can be used for analytics and reporting on service configuration.

This data product enables analytics teams to understand and report on the service configuration landscape without manually extracting data from the system. By connecting configuration data to SAP Business Data Cloud, organizations can accelerate time-to-insight for service management analytics, improve governance of their service configuration, and make faster, data-driven decisions about how their service processes are set up and optimized.

 

New BAdI in Recurring Service

Three new Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) have been introduced in the recurring service area to give customers greater control over how maintenance service plans generate call objects and service transactions:

  • BAdI – Validation on Scheduling (`CRMS4_MPLAN_SCHEDULING` / `CHECK_VALIDITY`): Controls whether a call object is generated at all, based on customer-defined criteria evaluated against the maintenance plan item and call data.
  • BAdI – Adding Service Items to an Existing Service Transaction (`CRMS4_MPLAN_CALL_TYPE_5` / `GET_SERVICE_TRANSACTION_REUSE`): Allows items from a new scheduling call to be added to an already-existing service transaction rather than always creating a new one.
  • BAdI – Enable Standard Contract Determination for Service Transaction Item (`CRMS4_MPLAN_ORDER_PREPARE` / `ALLOW_CONTRACT_DETERMINATION`): Enables standard contract determination to run at the item level of the generated service transaction.

These BAdIs give service organizations the extensibility needed to tailor recurring service scheduling to their specific operational rules — for example, suppressing call object generation based on equipment condition, consolidating multiple scheduling calls into a single service order, or ensuring the correct service contract is always determined at the item level. This reduces manual intervention in recurring service processes and supports more sophisticated, customer-specific maintenance automation.

entry.title: Upcoming Get Certified Live Session Series in April 2026
entry.content:

Below is an overview of Get Certified live session series starting in April 2026. Save the dates and register early! Please note that you require a valid SAP Learning Hub subscription to enroll and attend live sessions.

Get Certified: SAP Certified Associate – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Management Accounting 

🔗 Register here: 
https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-associate-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-management-accounting

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-end-to-end-business-processes-for-the-intelligent-enterprise

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (001) - SR(Service Request)作成時に確認しておきたいポイント
entry.content:
はじめに

S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionをご利用のお客様向けに、RISE側へ作業依頼を行う際に使用するService Request(以下、SR)のテンプレートの利用方法についてご紹介します。

SRの利用方法に先立ち、作業依頼前に確認された方が良い事項についてご案内いたします。
本ブログでは、SAP for Meを利用したSR作成方法を中心にご説明しております。

SRご利用前の確認事項

S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionをご利用のお客様が現時点で利用可能なSRテンプレートは100以上あり、多岐にわたります。
下記の内容を確認する事でRISE側に依頼可能な作業と各作業の内容がより明確になるかと考えます。
なお、ご不明な点などがございましたら、RISE担当者までにメールでご確認ください。


1. Roles and Responsibilities

S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionをご利用のお客様がRISE側へ依頼可能な作業内容は、「Roles and Responsibilities」にて定義されております。そのため、事前にご一読いただくことを推奨いたします。

また、「Roles and Responsibilities」は年2回改訂されており、3月にマイナー改訂、7月にメジャー改訂が行われます。

最新の「Roles and Responsibilities」は、以下のリンクよりご確認いただけます。
SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition - Service Specifications

本資料をご確認いただくことで、RISE側に依頼可能な作業と、お客様側で実施すべき作業の区別が明確になります。


2. SRのService Description

すべてのSRには、Service Description(該当サービスにおける制約事項や実施内容等)が定義されています。

例えば、SR「Remote Client Copy」のService Descriptionには、クライアントサイズが300GB以下であること、ならびに当該作業実施中にSPAM/SAINTおよびCLOUDLMアドオンのアップデートが実行されることが記載されています。


Service Descriptionをご確認いただくことで、該当SRの実施内容をより正確に把握でき、誤ったSRの作成によるプロジェクトスケジュールへの影響を未然に防ぐことが可能になるかと考えます。

3. SRの各項目のヘルプ

CSRの各項目には複数のオプションが用意されており、オプション値のみでは内容が明確に見えない場合もあるかと考えます。
その際は、「?(ヘルプ)」をクリックし、各オプションの詳細説明をご確認ください。



4. SR実施におけるのダウンタイム有無

SR作成画面にて"Download data"をクリックする事でSR Templateがリストされたファイルをダウンロードできます。




 

ダウンロードした"Services.xlsx"ファイルの"Estimated Standard Execution Time"項目をご確認願います。
Downtime: サービス停止ありの作業
Uptime: サービス停止なしの作業

下記は、ダウンロードしたSRテンプレートのサンプルとなります。

依頼したSR作業期間中に業務への影響有無(サービス停止有無)は、重要なポイントとなりますので、作業依頼の計画段階にてご確認する事をおすすめします。

5. SRにおける作業時間

SRでの作業時間は、過去の実績をもとに平均化した時間を基準として設定しております。
なお、作業が予定より早く完了した場合には、速やかにご連絡いたします。
作業時間は平均値に基づくものであるため、個別のSRにおいては長く感じられる場合がございますが、多くのお客様に安定したサービスを提供するための運用となっております。

注意事項

  1. SRテンプレートは、日々見直しと改善が行われておりますので、SR作成時に都度ご確認する事をおすすめします。
  2. お客様側でSRを作成した後、RISE側で準備作業/作業実施/事後作業を実施するのが基本的な流れとなります。
    なお、SR対応中に担当者がお客様への確認が必要と判断した場合、SRのステータスが"カスタマアクション"となります。
    SRのステータスが"カスタマアクション"になった場合、お客様からの返信がない場合、後続の作業が実施されないため、SRステータス変更を確認し返信する必要がございます。
    従って、SAP for Me上でSRのアップデート通知を設定する事を推奨します。

まとめ

SRをご利用いただくにあたり、確認した方が良いと考えられるポイントをご紹介しました。
S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionをご利用のお客様がSRを作成される際の一助となれば幸いです。

次回からは、よく利用されるSRテンプレートについてご紹介いたします。

entry.title: System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA: Downtime Reduction with Technical Options
entry.content:
Downtime is always a topic in most SAP S/4HANA conversion projects. From technical point of view, what can we do to reduce and optimize the downtime? Let me share my experiences with this topic as part of my work in the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team. 

1.   SAP S/4HANA Overview of Transition scenarios 

The transition to SAP S/4HANA is a strategic business transformation that involves moving from legacy systems like SAP ECC to SAP's modern, in-memory ERP platform. This transition is essential since SAP is planning to end standard support for SAP ECC in December 2027, with extended maintenance available until 2030. 

You may know the SAP S/4HANA overview of transition scenarios below. This blog will focus on SAP S/4HANA conversion scenarios only. 

2.   SAP S/4HANA Standard Conversion General Steps in downtime windows 

In a standard SAP S/4HANA conversion, the business downtime window is the period from when the system is unavailable for end users until it will become available for them again. 

Let’s look at the following general phases: 

  • System Ramp Down and Isolation: The process begins mainly with locking all users and stopping interfaces from/to external systems to ensure that no new data will be entered. Those are rather customer-specific steps, please always have your conversion delivery team align in detail with the customer IT and business teams. 
  • Software Update Manager (SUM) Downtime: SUM finalizes the conversion of SAP S/4HANA and switches the system to the new repository, this is the main part from technical point of view which we can optimize to reduce the total downtime. 
  • Finance (FI) Data Conversion: SUM converts FI table contents from the old SAP ERP data model to the new SAP S/4HANA data model. We treat it as a candidate for optimization, but we will not go into detail about it in this blog. 
  • SAP S/4HANA Technical and Application post-processing steps: Those steps are critical to finalize the system to get it ready again for business operations. These steps ensure that all functional requirements are covered with the new S/4HANA data models and that the system's technical infrastructure is stable. .System Validation and Ramp-up: Technical teams and key functional users verify core configurations and critical processes before releasing the system back to the end users. 

3.    Terminology and Approaches 

3.1   SAP S/4HANA Conversion Tools and Approaches (Conversion only) 

Approaches 

Scenarios and how to reduce the downtime 

Standard SUM/DMO 

Software Update Manager/Database Migration Option - Conversion with DB migration 

Depending on whether the source system is on an SAP HANA database or not, we will choose to use SUM or DMO by picking up different endpoints to launch for the conversion. There are general recommendations to work on to reduce the technical tool downtime periods. 

doDMO (Downtime Optimized Approach) 

Downtime-optimized Database Migration Option - Conversion with DB migration and downtime reduction (DB migration partly during uptime) 

doC (Downtime Optimized Approach) 

Downtime-optimized Conversion - Conversion with significant downtime reduction (DB migration partly and data conversion partly during uptime) 

DMO with System Move 

Conversion with DB migration and data center relocation 

DMOVE2S4  

DMO Move to SAP S/4HANA - Conversion with DB migration and data center relocation 

DMOVE2S4 with doDMO 

Combining DMOVE2S4 and doDMO – DC move, S/4HANA Conversion and reduce downtime by DB migration partly during uptime 

DMOVE2S4 with doC 

Combine DMOVE2S4 and doC  – DC move, S/4HANA Conversion and reduce downtime by DB migration and data conversion partly during uptime 

System Transition Workbench 

A guided tool for moving to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition includeing different scenarios 

NZDM 

near-Zero Downtime Maintenance (ABAP)  - Conversion from SAP Business Suite on HANA to SAP S/4HANA,  an advanced preconfiguration mode and feature in SUM with Record and Replay technique to reduce downtime   

NZDT 

Near-Zero Downtime Technology 

3.2 How to choose the most suitable conversion strategy and conversion path  

Scenario 

Source System 

Available Options 

On-Premise In-Place Conversion 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on non-HANA Database 

DMO, doDMO, doC 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on HANA Database 

SUM, nZDM, doDMO, doC 

Migration & Conversion to Hyperscalers / Customer's Other Data Center 

SAP ERP 6.0 on non-HANA Database 

DMO with System Move, DMOVE2S4, DMOVE2S4 with doDMO, DMOVE2S4 with doC 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on HANA Database 

DMO with System Move, DMOVE2S4, DMOVE2S4 with doDMO, DMOVE2S4 with doC 

Migration & Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on non-HANA Database 

DMO with System Move, DMOVE2S4, DMOVE2S4 with doDMO, DMOVE2S4 with doC, System Transition Workbench 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on HANA Database 

DMO with System Move, DMOVE2S4, DMOVE2S4 with doDMO, DMOVE2S4 with doC, System Transition Workbench 

What are the factors and criteria for the decision making with different options?  

From technical point of view, it is not a complicated question, see below decision tree. 

At the same time, you can refer to Road Map Viewer - SAP for Me for the general information 

From overall project perspective, there are some important factors such as: 

  • Total project timeline and cost; 
  • Downtime reduction; 
  • Technical prerequisites and functional prerequisites; 
  • Etc. and customer can define by themselves. Here is a delivery example about the evaluation of the approach and tools options with pros and cons based on customer’s requirements and SAP shared the proposal in a high-level picture. 

If still you have doubts, please reach out to your SAP professional consulting and service teams about additional recommendations for the different approaches based on the system data, sizing report, readiness reports and so onSome customers we worked with choose in-place SAP S/4HANA conversion in their data center and migration of SAP S/4HANA with Copy to target SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. If you choose this option, please consider the total business downtime. And right now, SAP provides this with featured option: RISE with System Transition Workbench with scenario: SUM/DMO run in local data center and use HANA tenant copy to achieve this. 

 3.3   RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench  

It provides a pre-planned “route” tailored to your starting point and destination, saving you the time to plan and reach the route. It gives real-time traffic updates and suggests alternative routes, helping you avoid congestion and delays. 

About "RISE with SAP system transition workbench" | SAP Help Portal  

SAP System Transition Workbench is installed by Enterprise Cloud Services(ECS) on a VM Migration Server. You can get seamless support from your SAP delivery team and SAP development teams as it is released and generally available to all customers. 

There is one great introduction blog for this tool from Kristin. 

Introducing the RISE with SAP System Transition Wo... - SAP CommunityThe tool can be used for migration of customer systems to SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) in context of RISE with SAP. 

  • SAP HANA Tenant Copy 
  • SAP HANA Backup and Recovery 
  • Homogeneous "Update and Copy" 
  • System Conversion using SUM 2.0 (DMO with System Move) 
  • System Conversion using SUM 2.0 (DMOVE2S4) 

Usage Restriction 

  • The above mentioned scenario is permitted for SAP ECS internal operations as well as for qualified migration partners. 
    Qualified migration partners must have earned at least the “Essential Level” designation for the SAP Cloud ERP Private competency as visible in SAP Partner Finder. 
  • SAP provides required automation tooling and configuration of the virtual machine for the migration.  
    Any non-authorized software export of the automation tooling or its configuration is prohibited. 
    Any usage beyond defined usage constraints is not allowed. 
  • The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench assumes that the target system and component releases are within SAP maintenance. Technical limitations and/or (general) restrictions might require additional preparation or an upgrade to newer releases of SAP S/4HANA related applications, add-ons, or other technical components. 
  • Currently, the RISE with SAP system transition workbench does not support SAP HANA scale-out target systems 

There is also the published success story of RISE with System Transition workbench helping from Piyush and Praveen to customer accelerated on their cloud journey to RISE.   

Accelerating RISE Migrations: CommScope’s Success Story with Transition Workbench Update and Copy - An Innovative migration approach for Rise 

3.4   Near-Zero Downtime Technology 

NZDT and doC are the two approaches as part of Minimized Downtime Services supporting all scenarios.  

As per SAP Note 693168 - Minimized Downtime Service (MDS) , doC is generally available to customers while NZDT is service-based and SAP Global Delivery Hub would have to be involved. Please always reach out to your SAP consulting team about NZDT scenarios since only a  limited number of customers can be supported. 

4.   Second level considerations and SUM/DMO optimizations 

4.1    Technical Downtime Optimization (TDO) app  

 To ensure that the maintenance using Software Update Manager on the production system goes smoothly, test cycles are performed before updating  production. And typically, the updated system is used to validate the upgrade process and identify any necessary preparation steps and fixes. 

To address these concerns, SAP offer the Technical Downtime Optimization App. This app provides multiple features for analyzing the Software Update Manager runtime and improving the maintenance event. 

 

You can refer to more details about the TDO usage: https://tdo.cloud.sap 

SAP Blog : Simply analyzing and improving maintenance events with Technical Downtime Optimization App 

4.2   General Recommendations to reduce technical downtime 

SAP Note 2351294 - S/4HANA System Conversion / Upgrade: Measures to reduce technical downtime 

4.3   General Recommendations on SAP HANA Database 

  • SAP HANA parameter changes will help data conversion performance but may impact productive performance. Therefore, if you apply these changes before entering the technical downtime, don’t forget to revert those changes after the completion of SUM. 
  • Increase the CPU capacity used for delta merges by changing the indexserver.ini parameter [mergedog] -> load_balancing_func value to “LCC / 2”. Remember the original value to restore it later. 
  • Increase the number of merge tokens and merge threads by setting indexserver.ini parameters [mergedog] -> token_per_table and [indexing] -> parallel_merge_threads to a value of available physical CPU cores divided by 2 (but no more than 256). 
  • Increase the parallelism for partitioned tables by setting indexserver.ini parameter [indexing] -> parallel_merge_part_threads to a value of available physical CPU cores divided by 2 (but no more than 256). If the parameter is not yet available in section [indexing], add it as a new parameter. 
  • Please monitor the memory usage of the HANA database server via visual load history of the HANA cockpit, or the relevant monitoring during the execution of critical phases. 

4.4   General recommendations on SUM/DMO parameters 

Setting up parallel processes properly can dramatically increase the performance of upgrades and updates of SAP systems. As best practice, the following should be considered: 

  • SAP Note 1616401 - Parallelism in the Upgrades, EhPs and Support Packages implementations 
  • Hardware resource utilization during Upgrades and Updates 

              SUM settings 1: ABAP PROCESSES (UPTIME) and ABAP PROCESSES (DOWNTIME) 

Number of parallel background processes and activator processes, also number of batch processes scheduled on the application server. The minimum number is 5. We recommend a value that is high enough to enable parallel processing, but small enough that the production system is not affected. 

              SUM settings 2: SQL PROCESSES (UPTIME) and SQL PROCESES (DOWNTIME) 

Number of parallel SQL processes used by tp to execute database statements. 

              SUM settings 3: R3TRANS PROCESSES (UPTIME) and R3TRANS PROCESSES (DOWNTIME) 

Number of parallel import processes used by R3trans to import data into the database. Note that these processes can generate significant loads on the database. 

              SUM settings 4: R3LOAD PROCESSES 

Number of R3load processes used for the EU_IMPORT phases. 

 

PHASES (SUM)

PARALLELISM MECHANISM

ACT_UPG

BATCH

DDIC_UPG

R3TRANS

PARCONV_UPG

SQL DDL

PARDIST_SHD

BATCH

SHADOW_IMPORT_INC

R3TRANS

SHADOW_IMPORT_UPG1

R3TRANS

SHADOW_IMPORT_UPG2

R3TRANS

TABIM_UPG

R3TRANS

XPRAS_AIMMRG

BATCH

EU_CLONE_MIG_DT/UT_RUN

R3load

 

4.5  The classic optimization methods 

  • Usually optimization is started with analysis of files SAPupConsole.log, LONGPOST LOG, UPGANA.XML and APPLANA.XML. 
  • DMO optimization: Benchmark tool for export/import: see blog Optimizing DMO Performance - SAP Community. 
  • Parallelization framework (SHDB_PFW_CONF) - based on hardware resources of the application server. 
  • SAP Note 2353814 - SAP S/4HANA conversion reports in SD: optimize parallel execution of conversion reports: . 
  • For AnyDB to SAP S/4HANA conversion: 
  • Duration files can be re-used and R3load migration logs are analyzed to check if better splitting can be determined, or a different order of packages. 
  • Source database statistics updates and, in some cases index rebuild as well as table reorganization can reduce the runtime. 

4.6    The steps and phases that contribute most during SUM/DMO downtime: 

  

Phase #  

Key Technical Steps 

Description 

1 

EU_CLONE_MIG_DT_EXP / IMP 

Export and Import: Application tables are migrated from the source database to the target HANA database. 

2 

PARCONV_UPG/ 

PARMVNT_APP_VIEWS 

Parallel Conversion: Converts application tables to the new release's structure and activates their nametabs. 

3 

TABIM_UPG 

Main Table Import: Imports remaining data into SAP-standard tables (classes S, W, E, C, G). 

4 

XPRAS_AIMMRG 

Execution of XPRAs: Runs Reports After Import (XPRAs) to adjust data and settings for the new system version. - Data conversion 

5 

JOB_KONV_MIGRATION/ 

RUN_VBFA_MIGRATION 

SFW - Shadow field Migration for KONV-PRCD_ELEMENTS Conversion 

6 

PARRUN_MKPF*_S4 / 

PARRUN_MKPF_VERIFY 

MM-IM conversion 

7 

PARRUN_FILL_EXTRACT_S4 

MM-IM Conversion 

8 

XPRAS_AIMMRG 

Execution of XPRAs: Runs Reports After Import (XPRAs) to adjust data and settings for the new system version. 

9 

Customer-specific XPRAs 

TABIM_UPG_CUST 

XPRAS_UPG_CUST 

Customer-specific XPRAs 

10 

RUN_SDMI_POST_PROCESSING 

Silent Data Migration: A post-processing step that migrates application data in the background after the system is back online, reducing technical downtime. 

4.7   PFW (SHDB Parallelization Framework) settings based on hardware resources of the App server 

SHDB Parallelization Framework  is and ABAP SAP_BASIS framework to simplify the parallelization topic for ABAP applications via PFW instances covering downtime conversion steps.              

The configuration table SHDB_PFW_CONF  

Report/ t_code:: SHDB_PDW_SUPPORT to configure and adjust the parameters or via scripts directly from sap note 2351294 - S/4HANA System Conversion / Upgrade: Measures to reduce technical downtime 

If still you have doubts, please reach out to your SAP professional consulting and service teams about additional recommendations for the different approaches based on the system data, sizing report, readiness reports and so on. 

5. Summary and Conclusion 

Optimizing downtime for an SAP S/4HANA conversion is not just about choosing the right tool; it is about a multi-layered approach that combines strategy, infrastructure tuning, and precise tool configuration. 

By selecting the most appropriate transition scenario (Standard, doDMO, or doC), leveraging the Technical Downtime Optimization (TDO) app, and fine-tuning parallelism within SUM and the HANA database, you can significantly shrink the business impact of your cutover. 

Key Takeaways: 

  • Analyze Early: Use the TDO app and previous mock run logs to identify bottlenecks. 
  • Tune the Engine: Don't settle for default SUM parameters; adjust ABAP and SQL processes based on your hardware. 
  • Leverage Modern Tools: Consider and compare the tools accelerate the journey. 

Ultimately, every system is unique. We highly recommend performing multiple test conversions to validate these optimizations and ensure a smooth, predictable go-live. 

If you have other questions / feedback, please let me know in the comments. 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (002) - OSの最新セキュリティパッチ適用
entry.content:
OSに最新のセキュリティパッチを適用し、システムの安全性を維持することは、システム運用において重要なタスクの一つです。

本記事では、SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionをご利用の際に、OSの最新セキュリティパッチ適用を依頼する方法をご紹介します。

 

未適用のOSセキュリティパッチの確認

SAP for Meへログイン後、以下の手順で確認できます。
「Services & Support」→「Private Cloud Workspace」→「Risk Management」セクションの「Security Patches」→「Operating System」タブを選択し、対象のSIDをクリックしてください。

また、以下のURLへアクセスすることで、適用が必要なセキュリティパッチを確認することも可能です。
URL: https://me.sap.com/privcloudsecuritypatches#?variant=standard 

下記は、サンプル画面となります。

 

利用するSRテンプレート

OSのセキュリティパッチ適用を依頼する際は、以下のSRテンプレートを使用します。
SRテンプレート：Apply latest Security Patch to OS (Linux, Windows)

下記は、本記事作成時点におけるSR作成画面です。

 

Creating with Advance Scheduling：NO　※特別な理由がない限り、デフォルト値のままご使用ください。

Set system messages(for ABAP systems): ABAPシステムにおいて、メンテナンス期間中に表示するシステムメッセージを設定するかどうかを指定する項目です。


注意事項

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionでは、以下のように複数のシステムが提供されています。

  • SAP Cloud Connector
  • Web Dispatcher
  • S/4HANA（AP Server + SAP HANA）
    など

SAP HANAのSIDを選択してSRを作成した場合、AP ServerおよびSAP HANAのOSに対してセキュリティパッチが適用されます。
一方で、SAP Cloud ConnectorおよびWeb Dispatcherについては、それぞれのSIDごとにSRを作成する必要があります。


まとめ

システムを脆弱性から保護するため、OSのセキュリティパッチの定期的な適用を推奨いたします。
なお、適用作業にはダウンタイムが伴うため、事前にメンテナンス時間を確保した上で、計画的に実施することをご検討ください。

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (003) - OSのアップデートとアップグレード
entry.content:
システムのOSは、End of Life(以下、EOL)を迎える前にバージョンを更新することが、システム運用において重要なタスクの一つです。

OSバージョンがEOLを過ぎると、新しいセキュリティパッチが提供されないこと、またテクニカルサポートが十分に提供されないことから、SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private EditionではTop Issueとしてもお客様にOSバージョンの更新を依頼しております。

本記事では、SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionをご利用の際のOSバージョン管理についてご紹介します。


SAP for Meにて現在利用中のOSバージョンを確認する方法

SAP for Meへログイン後、以下の手順で確認できます。
「Services & Support」→「Private Cloud Workspace」→「Landscape Management」セクションの「My Systems」をクリックします。

また、以下のURLへアクセスすることで、お客様のシステムリストを確認することも可能です。
https://me.sap.com/privcloudlandscape2#?variant=standard

特定SIDのOSバージョンは、対象システムのSIDをクリックし、Factsheetタブにて確認できます。

下記はサンプル画面となります。

 

全システムの情報は、［Download Tables］をクリックすることで確認できます。

ダウンロードしたExcelファイルの「OS Version」または「OS Release」列をご確認ください。

 

サポートされるOSバージョン

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private EditionでサポートされるOSバージョンは、SUSEのリリースとは異なります。
理由は、SUSEで新しいバージョンがリリースされた後に、RISE側で該当バージョンへの更新手順の確立および動作確認が必要となるためです。

そのため、該当SRテンプレートで選択可能なサービスパックおよびOSバージョンが基準となることをご承知おきください。

すべての作業に共通しますが、開発・品質保証・本番の順序で作業を計画し、ビジネスシナリオをお客様側でテストすることで、本番環境の安定稼働を確保することを推奨します。

リリース済みのSUSEバージョンおよびEOLは、下記SUSE公式サイトでご確認いただけます。
RISEでのEOLは、SUSE公式サイトに掲載されている「LTSS Ends」と同様です。

Product Support Lifecycle | SUSE

 

下記は、SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15のサポート期限の例です。

 

利用するSRテンプレート

SRにてOSのアップデート／アップグレードをご紹介する前に、用語の定義を行います。

  • アップデート: メジャーバージョンは変更せず、マイナーバージョン（サービスパック）を更新すること。
    例：SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 → SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP5
  • アップグレード: メジャーバージョンを変更すること。
    例：SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 12 → SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15

なお、本記事に記載のSR作成画面は、記事作成時点のものです

 

OSのアップデート

SRテンプレート: Update OS Service Pack (Linux)

  • Current OS version: ご利用中のバージョンが自動的に表示されます。
  • Target Service Pack version: アップデートのターゲットバージョンを選択してください。
  • Set system message (for ABAP systems):  ABAPシステムにおいて、メンテナンス期間中に表示するシステムメッセージを設定するかどうかを指定する項目です。

 

 

OSのアップグレード

SRテンプレート: Upgrade SLES OS to Major Version

 

  • Current OS version: ご利用中のバージョンが自動的に表示されます。
  • Target OS version: アップデートのターゲットバージョンを選択してください。
  • Set system message (for ABAP systems):  ABAPシステムにおいて、メンテナンス期間中に表示するシステムメッセージを設定するかどうかを指定する項目です。

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private EditionでサポートされるSUSEの最新メジャーバージョンは現時点で15のため、Target OS versionでは選択可能な項目がありません。

 

まとめ

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionでは、OSバージョンをEOL前に更新することが重要です。
OSの更新には、サービスパック更新の「アップデート」とメジャーバージョン更新の「アップグレード」があります。
現在のOSバージョンはSAP for Meで確認でき、サポート対象OSバージョンはSAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionで定められております。
更新作業は開発・品質保証・本番環境の順序で計画し、テストを実施することで本番環境の安定稼働を確保することが推奨されます。

entry.title: Custom Business Configurations (F4579): Collaborative draft
entry.content:
Introduction

The Custom Business Configurations (CUBCO) app serves as an entry point to the Business Configuration Maintenance Object (SMBC) provided by custom applications or partners.

When you use the ADT wizard to generate a Business Configuration Maintenance Object, the singleton pattern is used when generating the draft enabled RAP BO. One characteristic of this pattern is that all child entities are locked together, so it is not possible for multiple users to work with the same configuration at the same time.

This blog describes how to avoid this restriction by using collaborative draft.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (2025)
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example scenario

In the configuration table ZDEMO_CC, the company codes are stored.

@EndUserText.label : 'Company Code'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_cc {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key company_code      : abap.numc(4) not null;
  country               : land1;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Enable collaborative draft

In the generated behavior definition, add "collaborative" to the draft annotation:

managed with additional save implementation in class ZBP_I_COMPANYCODE_S unique;
strict;
with collaborative draft;

Add the draft query annotation and the share action to the singleton entity definition:

define behavior for ZI_CompanyCode_S alias CompanyCodeAll
draft table ZDEMO_CC_D_S query ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
[...]
{
  draft action Share;
  [...]
}

 Right-click the singleton draft table and choose New Data Definition to create the CDS view for the draft query:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Draft Query View'
define view entity ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
  as select from zdemo_cc_d_s
{
  key singletonid                   as Singletonid,
      lastchangedatmax              as Lastchangedatmax,
      transportrequestid            as Transportrequestid,
      draftentitycreationdatetime   as Draftentitycreationdatetime,
      draftentitylastchangedatetime as Draftentitylastchangedatetime,
      draftadministrativedatauuid   as Draftadministrativedatauuid,
      draftentityoperationcode      as Draftentityoperationcode,
      hasactiveentity               as Hasactiveentity,
      draftfieldchanges             as Draftfieldchanges
}

You need to enhance the authorization of the involved users. In SAP BTP ABAP environment and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition you assign business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_RAP_DRAFT_PC to the user, in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition authorization object S_DRAFT.

Collaboration

User TB is creating a new company code 400 while at the same time user PW is editing company code 100. All changes are recorded on the same transport request.

Since the collaborative draft is enabled for the singleton root entity, the save and discard draft action are applied to all object changes. In this example, users PW and TB process two different company codes, which are logically different objects. Technically, however, they process the same singleton root entity. In practice, this means that discarding the draft or saving must be aligned between the users involved, as the save action persists the changes in all company codes.
For completely independent editing of individual objects, you must develop a RAP BO without a singleton pattern.

entry.title: Spot Tender in SAP S/4 HANA 2025+ with Lean Service based Settlement. (Default Charge Type)
entry.content:
This blog post will help readers, customers, and SAP TM users familiarize themselves with the SAP Transportation Management’s Spot procurement functionality in SAP S/4 HANA 2025 with Lean Service based Transportation Settlement.

SAP Transportation Management’s spot procurement is a subset of the Tendering process having 2 broad categories i.e. Contracted and non-contracted carrier.

In my previous blog post I highlighted step-by-step process for one of the many ways in which we can carry out spot tender in version below S/4 HANA TM 2025. Going forward the process has evolved compared to previous version and also based on the business needs and processes.

Below is the link to PART 1 of Spot Tender which clearing calls out detailed process steps:

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-members/spot-tender-in-sap-s-4hana-transportation-management/ba-p/13541617

You can read more about SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management -Tendering in the Application Help for SAP S/4HANA
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/c336c907db3742eeb97e9138dd419970.html?locale=en-US

Note: I have carried out the demonstration with relevant screen shot from an internal system for ease of understanding. There’s no relevance or relation to any external source.

Use Case

Use case is for both the customers in Transportation & Logistics Industry (T&L) as well as shippers from Retail, CPG, manufactures etc. who procure services to move their loads or are in business of providing transportation services and have implemented SAP S/4 HANA 2025+ with Lean service procurement.

Assumption: 

The customer is a CPG company and implemented SAP S/4HANA 2025 SAP Transportation Management along with Lean service procurement.

They have yearly contracts with the carriers to move certain capacities on a given corridor/ route. Alternatively, they may also reach out to carriers who don’t have a contract. Contract in the SAP TM world should be referred as FRA (Freight Agreement).

Challenge:

During certain festive seasons the demand on a particular corridor may outweigh the capacity already procured during the start of the year. In this situation, it becomes really very important to meet the increasing demand and ship the goods to our esteemed customers within the stipulated time frame.

Towards the end of the year the procurement team along with Finance would like to analyze the premium paid as transportation services and how it can be curtailed in the upcoming fiscal year. Alternatively, For many years clients have been requesting to book premium cost in a different G/L and replace the std ADJ charge into a more business defined terminology.

In an ideal world, in the absence of the above the cost segregation is cumbersome and involves additional resources and efforts which aren’t quantified. For the leadership as well, this seems to be a non-transparent process with certainly being a  post facto process to adjust the book of records.

Solution:

SAP S/4 HANA TM 2025+ with Lean Service procurement has come out with an out of the box provision to modify the std ADJ charge type during spot tender process which provides step-by-step visibility and more opportunity to drive the tender process both from process as well as financial stand point. (*some limitations and observations shared in the trail blog post)

Scenario:

Let’s move directly at the stage where a FRO is created and subcontracted. Further, we have received a response for an RFQ from the carrier with evaluation completed. We will begin from the step where the user has decided to manually award the carrier.

We start with an assumption that all Tender relevant configs are in place, as the focus is not on setting up the system but on functionality walk-through.

Pictorial representation of the demonstrated functionality:

Basic Tender flow (Representation purpose)

Post Award system will check whether a valid active contract exists or not. (Representation purpose)

Post Award system will check whether a valid active contract exists or not. (Representation purpose)

Note: Let us now consider two scenarios in the case of Award quotation:

  1. Awarding a carrier with whom we don’t have a Freight Agreement.
  2. Awarding a carrier with whom we have a Freight Agreement.

Scenario 1: Awarding a carrier with whom we don’t have a Freight Agreement.
 
Step 1: User is trying to award a carrier and stumbled upon below messages.

The system has hindered the carrier award process and will not allow to proceed further.

Let us analyze how to can overcome the errors.

Step 2: In order to resolve above error, we can be leverage below BADI and implementation as showcased below.

BADI: /SCMTMS/TCC_TEND_DFLT_CHRG_TYP

Here you can observe that I have created a new charge type ZADITYA to replace ADJ. (Charge type created for representation purpose only)

The glaring difference compared to previous blog/version is that system now restricts and aborts the award process which wasn’t the case earlier.

Step 3: Let us try to award the carrier Post BADI implementation.

The popped messages don’t hinder with the carrier award process and carrier is successfully awarded.

Step 4: When we observe the Charges tab, awarded carrier submitted a price of 4500 USD and system has assigned the entire spot tender amount i.e. 4500 USD earlier viewed under Adjusted/Quoted Amount charge type to ZADITYA.

You can further map the Charge type to different G/L to apportion the cost accordingly. (* Not part of this blog Post)

Now let us look at the Flip side i.e. Awarding Carrier having an FRA (Freight Agreement)

Scenario 2:  Awarding a carrier with whom we have a Freight Agreement.

Note: The BADI implementation is still active.

Step 5:Post awarding the carrier, since system determined an FRA, it displayed below messages with no visible warnings nor errors.

Now let’s Analyze the Charge Tab.

Step 6: In this case, the awarded carrier submitted a price of 4500 USD and has an existing agreement with charges amounting to 2000 USD. System subtracts agreement charges from the submitted charges i.e. giving a clear picture of the individual charges incurred.

The glaring difference to note in contacted scenario is that system didn’t replace the ADJ charge type but continues to work as previous version.

You will have to continue to apportion premium charges as part of ADJ charge type with 2 different ways of handling the process for the customer having both active scenarios.

*Will share more insights but at this stage the above is the observed system behavior.

The demonstration in this blog post doesn’t focus on the configs to setup the system nor does it highlight the further freight settlement process.

Hope this article helps all of us understand the upcoming new functionality of the spot tender process in the SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management 2025 with Lean service procurement. There may be multiple different ways to carry out the tendering process but, in this demonstration, I chose to keep it generic and simple.

Thank you for taking time and effort to read this.

Since the tests are carried out with limited resources and purely based on observations, any Feedback/comments in any form will be highly appreciated.

SAP Cloud Transport Management SAP Transportation Management Travel and Transportation SAP S/4HANA SAP Business Network for Logistics

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q1-26 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
As we continue into 2026, I’m back with the next edition of my quarterly blog series covering the latest innovations and highlights in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Over the last quarter, we’ve extended existing capabilities, introduced new ones, and invested further in usability, integration, and enablement – all with the goal of helping our customers calculate, analyze, and operationalize sustainability footprints at scale, grounded in ERP data. In this edition, I’m covering:

  • Transport Footprint Enhancements
  • Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)
  • Distance Adjustment Factors
  • Transport Analytics
  • Categorize Scope 3.2 Footprints
  • Usability Improvements
  • Other Release Highlights
  • Unibloom Emission Factor Datasets
  • Multi‑Impact Footprints in Practice: Learning Journey, FLAG and TfS Use Cases
  • Additional Information

Transport Footprint Enhancements

The last quarter brought several enhancements to the ERP‑centric transport footprint calculation capabilities in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. These updates extend coverage to customer-relevant transport footprints, including integration of customer master data, give you more control over the distance calculations used for transport footprints, and provide you with comprehensive analytical capabilities.

Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)

In one of my previous posts, I introduced ERP‑centric transport footprint calculation for upstream and internal transport, covering Greenhouse Gas Protocol Scope 3.4 emissions. This capability has now been extended to the downstream side of the value chain, enabling the calculation of customer‑relevant transport footprints and, with that, Scope 3.9 (Downstream Transport and Distribution) emissions.

The calculation also leverages existing ERP business transaction data and builds on the same transport modeling approach used for upstream transport. The foundation of this enhancement is the introduction of a Customer role for business partners. Business partners with this role, including address data, can be replicated directly from integrated SAP S/4HANA source systems, through APIs, or imported via file in the Import Master Data app. The system derives customer‑relevant transport activities from the following business transactions:

  • Goods Issue for Customer (Outbound deliveries of products to customers)
  • Customer Return (Goods returned by customers to company locations).

When business transactions are replicated from integrated source systems, these transactions now include the Customer ID, ensuring correct assignment of customer‑relevant transport activities during transport footprint calculation. In this context, targeted enhancements were made to the Import Master Data and Import Business Transactions OData APIs on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Based on the configurations in the Manage Transport Settings app, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management automatically generates transport activities, determines routes and legs, and calculates footprints per activity, route, and leg, applying the relevant emission factors by mode of transport. The resulting Scope 3.9 emissions are seamlessly integrated into both Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) and Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) calculations and are available in the standard footprint result and analytical apps – all grounded in ERP data rather than manual estimates.

For setup and process details, see Calculating Customer‑Relevant Transport Footprints Using Business Transactions in SAP Help. For importing business partners and business transactions, check Configurations for Importing Master Data and Configurations for Importing Business Transaction Data.

Customer Relevant Transport Footprints (Scope 3.9)

Distance Adjustment Factors

Transport footprint calculations in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management derive distances between locations automatically using the Great Circle Distance (GCD) method. Since real transport routes often deviate from the shortest path due to road networks, infrastructure, and routing constraints, GCD can underestimate the actual distance traveled. To reflect this, you can now configure distance adjustment factors for different modes of transport in the Manage Transport Settings app. When routes and legs are generated, the system applies the configured adjustment factor to the GCD, resulting in a more realistic distance basis for footprint calculations.

Before configuring distance adjustment factors, you should review the methodology documentation of your emission factor sources. Some emission factor datasets already include distance adjustments – for example, GLEC v3.1 emission factors for air transport. Applying an additional adjustment factor in such cases would lead to incorrect results.

Also note that existing transport activities aren’t updated automatically. To apply new distance adjustment factors, routes and legs must be regenerated and the relevant transport footprints recalculated. For details, check SAP Help: Configuring Distance Adjustment Factors and Generating Routes and Legs.

Configure Distance Adjustment Factors

Transport Analytics

To complement the transport footprint calculation capabilities, a new analytics app called Transport is now available in the Analytics section of the SAP Fiori launchpad. The app provides a comprehensive, map‑based view of corporate and product transport footprints for a selected footprint inventory scope and period. Transport legs are visualized as connections between source and target locations, with color‑coding that highlights higher‑emission legs. You can filter by product, mode of transport, business transaction type, or location, and switch to a table view for detailed, leg‑level analysis sorted by emission value. The app also supports comparisons across different periods. To use the app, you need the SUS_PFM_Calculate_Transport_Footprints_Read role. See SAP Help for more information: Analyzing Footprints for Transport.

Transport Analytics

To reflect all those updates, the process steps for calculating transport footprints using business transaction data have also been updated in the Scenario Guide: Process 8 – Calculating Scope 3.4 and Scope 3.9 Emissions Using Business Transaction Data.

 

Categorize Scope 3.2 Footprints

Until now, all purchased product footprints covered in the Manage Purchased Product Footprints app were automatically attributed to GHG Protocol Scope 3.1 (Purchased Goods and Services) in corporate carbon footprint calculations. For most companies, this is the correct assignment – but not in all cases. Capital goods such as machinery, equipment, or buildings that are purchased and used over multiple years belong to Scope 3.2 (Capital Goods). Correctly distinguishing between Scope 3.1 and Scope 3.2 is important both for GHG Protocol compliance and for maintaining a high‑quality footprint inventory. This is also a feature that was requested by customers via SAP Influence.

With a recent enhancement, you can now explicitly assign a purchased product footprint or product group footprint to either:

  • Scope 3.1 – Purchased Goods and Services, or
  • Scope 3.2 – Capital Goods

This categorization is supported consistently across all relevant workflows in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management:

  • When creating purchased product footprints manually,
  • When mapping emission factors manually or using AI‑based mapping, and
  • When importing purchased product footprints via data files.

Scope 3.2 Categorization

This enhancement ensures that each purchased product footprint is correctly attributed when calculating your organizational footprint inventory, resulting in a more accurate and auditable split between Scope 3.1 and Scope 3.2. Existing purchased product footprints that were previously categorized automatically remain assigned to Scope 3.1 by default, ensuring backward compatibility. For more information, see Creating Footprints for Purchased Products and Product Groups in SAP Help.

For analyzing your calculated footprints, the updated categorization is also reflected correctly in the Footprint Overview app, where purchased product footprints assigned to Scope 3.2 are shown separately from Scope 3.1.

 

Usability Improvements

To support more efficient day‑to‑day work with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we continuously invest in improving usability and increasing automation. Last quarter, we introduced several enhancements around form‑based data collection, manual data imports, and better transparency in integrations. These improvements focus on reducing manual effort in recurring data collection tasks, simplifying bulk operations, and making changes and corrections easier to manage at scale – especially in scenarios with large numbers of forms, users, or integrated systems. Many of these enhancements are the direct result of customer feedback, collected through SAP Influence as well as ongoing conversations with customers and partners.

You can now change the status of GHG data forms directly during data import when using spreadsheet templates in the Record GHG Data and Manage GHG Data Collections apps. GHG forms can be set to Applicable or Not Applicable as part of the import. When setting a form to Not Applicable, a comment is required, consistent with the behavior in the user interface. This applies to both bulk templates on the main page of the apps and the individual templates per GHG form type, streamlining bulk updates and reducing manual follow‑up steps.

To further automate and improve correction workflows, GHG data forms that were previously set to Completed or Not Applicable can now be reopened via API using the POST/massUpdate action in the GHG Data Forms API on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This provides additional flexibility for automated correction scenarios and integration workflows where reported data needs to be revised after submission.

For providing a more consistent and efficient user experience, downloaded templates now better reflect the UI and applied filters. Template sorting now mirrors the UI table sorting, and templates can be downloaded together with the currently applied UI filters. These enhancements apply to apps such as Manage Planned Energy Consumption Rates, Manage Business Activities, Model Energy Flows, Manage Waste Footprints, Record GHG Data, Manage Transport Operation Footprints, Manage Allocations, Manage Purchased Product Footprints, and Manage Transport Settings. In the Calculate Footprints, Model Energy Flows, Record GHG Data, and Manage GHG Data Collections apps, downloading templates together with the displayed data is now the default behavior.

Finally, the sustainability data integration apps now include a Change History tab in Manage Replications, Manage Source Systems, and Configure Extensions for Replications. This allows you to track who made which changes and when, including the old and new values for edited fields, improving transparency and traceability when managing replication configurations.

 

Other Release Highlights

When performing a current period calculation, input items for footprint items of type Product that only contribute a defined CO₂e value without a product quantity are no longer considered in the per‑unit calculation. These input items are excluded because they have no associated product quantity and would otherwise distort per‑unit results, for example transports or allocations. Their CO₂e values are still fully aggregated into the total footprint. When using CO₂e‑only input items, make sure they’re scaled to the total product quantity where appropriate.

The system now supports goods receipt from production even without production history such as goods issues to production, work‑in‑progress postings, or internal service confirmations in the current period. In these cases, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management uses the previous period footprint per unit of the produced product or, if not available, maintained purchased product footprints. This eliminates previous calculation errors and applies the product footprint determination methods defined in the calculation variant.

Custom temperature conditions can now be replicated from source systems using the Manage Replications app. This applies to SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition source systems. For pull‑based replication scenarios, the setup requires custom CDS views in the source system.

Lastly, we’re also continuously enhancing our documentation on SAP Help to improve transparency and onboarding. The Implement – Getting Started section now includes a link to ERP Objects, providing details on relevant CDS views, tables, and fields in SAP S/4HANA for each supported master data entity. In addition, the Product Carbon Footprint Scenario Guide has been extended with step‑by‑step processes for calculating product carbon footprints for cradle‑to‑gate and cradle‑to‑grave scenarios, including concrete examples for allocating Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions using business activities, allocations, and business transaction data.

 

Unibloom Emission Factor Datasets

One of the persistent challenges in Scope 3 reporting is the gap between supplier‑specific data and the global average emission factors that many companies fall back on when that data isn’t available.
